function_overview.content: Provides a read-only query interface for all internal modules to resolve instrument identity against the canonical entity table — supporting lookup by canonical key and attribute-based search with role-dependent status filtering.

roles.status_filter: All status types — Active, Inactive, Disabled
All status types — Active, Inactive, Disabled
Active by default; Inactive allowed if explicitly specified — Disabled never returned

preconditions.description: Canonical entity table is initialised and accessible
Caller is authenticated and their role is determinable at query time
Canonical records exist — created via PSD-0001 (Canonical Maintenance)

dependencies.description: PSD-0001 (Canonical Maintenance) — canonical records must exist before queries can return results
FRD-NMD-0002 source configuration schema — source identifier lookup (FR-NMD-001-011) is out of scope for this PSD and will be specified under FRD-NMD-0002

flows.name: Lookup by Canonical Key
flows.trigger: ET-006: Internal module requests canonical lookup by canonical key

inputs.notes: Optional — role-dependent; omit to use default (Active only)

processing_steps: 2. Resolve caller role and permitted status filter
3. Query canonical table by exact canonical_key match
4. Apply status filter per caller role
5. Return full canonical record or NOT_FOUND

outputs.success: Full canonical record — all 5 hierarchy columns, canonical_key, asset_class, entity_type, status, source, created_by, created_at, last_updated_by, last_updated_at

flows.sla: Hot path — called by every ingestion run; must meet platform latency target (NFR-NMD-001-001, TBD)

flows.name: Attribute Search
flows.trigger: ET-007: Caller searches canonical by attribute

inputs.notes: Fuzzy ticker search — prefix match only
Optional — role-dependent; omit to use default (Active only)

processing_steps: 1. Validate at least one search attribute is provided
2. Resolve caller role and permitted status filter
3. Build AND filter query across all provided attributes; apply prefix match on contract if supplied
4. Apply status filter per caller role
5. Sort results: Active first, then Inactive, then Disabled; then by exchange, commodity, product_type, product_code
6. Apply pagination — default page size 50, maximum 2000

outputs.success: Paginated list of matching canonical records — full record per item; empty list if no matches
outputs.pagination: Default page size: 50; maximum page size: 2000

flows.sla: Non-hot path — UI and export use; SLA TBD (OQ-002)

out_of_scope.reason: Source identifier resolution requires source-to-canonical mapping owned by FRD-NMD-0002 — to be specified in a PSD under FRD-NMD-0002 (FR-NMD-001-011)

rules.rule: Active records are returned by default; Inactive records returned only when caller explicitly requests them
Disabled records are never returned to Query & Export Module regardless of filter parameter
A NOT_FOUND result is returned when no matching record exists — the query never auto-creates a canonical record
Every query response includes the full canonical record: all 5 hierarchy columns, canonical_key, status, and audit metadata
Attribute search requires at least one attribute — open-ended queries with no filter are not permitted
Caller identity determines the permitted status filter — callers cannot override their role's status access boundary
Attribute search sort order is fixed: status ascending (Active → Inactive → Disabled), then exchange, commodity, product_type, product_code
Attribute search results are paginated — default page size 50, maximum 2000; requests exceeding 2000 are capped at 2000
Contract ticker search uses prefix match only — contains or suffix match not supported in Phase 1
Must be non-empty for Flow A
At least one of exchange, commodity, product_type, product_code, contract, entity_type must be provided for Flow B
rules.error_message: At least one search attribute is required

rules.rule: Query & Export Module may not request Disabled records
rules.error_message: Status filter not permitted for caller role

criteria.criterion: Given a caller submits a valid canonical_key, When Flow A executes, Then the matching record is returned in full or NOT_FOUND if no match exists
Given the same canonical_key is submitted by two different callers, When queried, Then both receive identical records subject to their respective status filters
Given a canonical_key lookup with no status filter specified, When the query runs, Then only Active records are returned by default
Given a Data Ingestion Module submits a lookup for a Disabled record, When the query runs, Then the Disabled record is returned
Given a Query & Export Module requests Disabled records explicitly, When the query runs, Then the request is rejected with 'Status filter not permitted for caller role'
Given a caller submits an attribute search with at least one attribute, When Flow B executes, Then all matching records are returned subject to the caller's status filter
Given a caller submits a contract prefix search, When Flow B executes, Then all records with contract values beginning with the supplied prefix are returned
Given an attribute search is submitted with no attributes, When validation runs, Then the request is rejected with 'At least one search attribute is required'
Given a Query & Export Module submits an attribute search with no status flag, When the query runs, Then only Active records are returned
Given a Query & Export Module submits an attribute search with Inactive flag specified, When the query runs, Then Active and Inactive records are returned — Disabled records excluded
Given an attribute search returns results, When the response is built, Then records are sorted Active first, then Inactive, then Disabled, then by exchange, commodity, product_type, product_code
Given an attribute search returns more than 50 records with no page size specified, When the response is built, Then only the first 50 records are returned
Given a caller requests a page size greater than 2000, When the query runs, Then the result set is capped at 2000 records

assumptions.description: Caller identity is determinable at query time — the canonical module can resolve which role a caller maps to
Source identifier lookup (FR-NMD-001-011) is out of scope for this PSD — to be specified in a PSD under FRD-NMD-0002

constraints.description: Read-only — no writes occur in this function
Query & Export Module is restricted to Active and Inactive results only — Disabled records never returned regardless of filter
Attribute search requires at least one input — unrestricted open queries are not permitted
Contract ticker search is prefix match only — contains or suffix match not supported in Phase 1
Attribute search results are paginated — default page size 50, maximum 2000
Attribute search sort order is fixed: status ascending (Active → Inactive → Disabled), then exchange, commodity, product_type, product_code

questions.question: What is the response time SLA for attribute search distinct from the hot-path lookup SLA (NFR-NMD-001-001)?

entries.author: Leo
entries.description: Initial draft — PROCESS_FLOW spec for FA-02 Canonical Query from FRD-NMD-0001. Covers lookup by canonical key (FLOW-A) and attribute search (FLOW-B) with role-dependent status filtering, prefix match on contract ticker, fixed sort order, and pagination (default 50, max 2000). Source identifier lookup (FR-NMD-001-011) explicitly out of scope — deferred to FRD-NMD-0002.
